channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The Latest: Teacher and a student killed in a shooting at a private Christian school in Wisconsin
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — A teenage student opened fire at the Abundant Life Christian School in Madison, Wisconsin on Monday, killing a teacher and another teenage student in the final week before Christmas break. The shooter also died, police said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former TV host Carlos Watson gets nearly 10 years in prison in case about failed startup Ozy Media
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former talk show host Carlos Watson was sentenced Monday to nearly 10 years in prison in a federal financial conspiracy case that cast his once-buzzy Ozy Media as an extreme of fake-it-'til-you-make-it startup culture.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Stage spoofs of Hallmark TV movies are Christmas gifts for theaters
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — A career woman from the big city gets stranded in a quaint town just before Christmas but somehow finds love on the holiday with a prince in disguise or a high school crush ... or a widowed father.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Things to know about the shooting at a Christian school in Wisconsin
item.description: Two people were killed and others were injured Monday in a shooting at a private Christian school in Madison, Wisconsin. The shooting happened days before the Christmas break. Police said the student who opened fire was also dead.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lt. Governor says Texas may purchase border wall material auctioned by federal government
item.description: The Texas Facilities Commission said the materials up for sale were "mostly junk, with most panels covered in concrete and rust.
item.dc:creator: Dylan McKim, NewsNation

item.title: Senate Democrats push plan to abolish Electoral College
item.description: Three Democratic senators unveiled a constitutional amendment to abolish the Electoral College system Monday, just more than a month after President-elect Trump stunned the Democrats by sweeping all seven battleground states, knocking off three Senate Democratic incumbents in the process. Sens.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Bud Light no longer the best-selling draft beer at bars, data suggests
item.description: The overtaking of Bud Light as America's most popular draft beer has been "years in the making," according to the founder of Draftline Technologies.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Missouri's death row had nearly 100 inmates in the 1990s. Now, it has 8
item.description: ST. LOUIS (AP) — Missouri 's status as one of the most active death penalty states is about to change for one simple reason: The state is running out of inmates to execute. The lethal injection of Christopher Collings on Dec.
item.dc:creator: JIM SALTER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Drone detectors in New Jersey have found 'little or no evidence' of wrongdoing, governor says
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (AP) — Drone-detecting devices deployed in New Jersey in the past week have found “little to no evidence” of anything nefarious or threatening, Gov.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Is that a drone or a plane? Experts help explain the differences
item.description: Up in the sky, is that a drone, a plane or a helicopter? Experts who study unmanned aircraft systems — better known as drones — say it can be tough to tell from miles away. But there are clues.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER and MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How to contact the Rhode Island cyberattack call center after data breach
item.description: Rhode Island officials urged residents to take immediate steps to protect themselves on Saturday, warning that the hackers could release personal data.
item.dc:creator: Leah Crowley, NewsNation

item.title: Bill to officially make bald eagle national bird heads to Biden’s desk
item.description: A bill to officially designate the bald eagle as the national bird will head to President Biden’s desk after passage in the House on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Jill Jacobson, actress who appeared on 'Star Trek' and 'Falcon Crest,' dies at 70
item.description: "We are incredibly sad to say goodbye to our beautiful, soulful, hysterically funny, elegantly raunchy client, Jill Jacobson," her manager said.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, NewsNation

item.title: Man sentenced for helping pregnant Chinese women travel to give birth in the US
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A California man was sentenced Monday to nearly 3 1/2 years in prison for running an extensive business that helped pregnant Chinese women travel to the United States to deliver babies who would automatically have American citizenship. U.S.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Teacher and student killed, 6 injured in Wisconsin school shooting
item.description: A teacher and a student were killed in a shooting at the Abundant Life Christian School in Madison, Wisconsin, police said Monday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Devin Willems, ABC 4

item.title: Teacher and a teenage student killed in a shooting at a Christian school in Wisconsin
item.description: A shooting at a Wisconsin Christian school occurred in a study hall and was reported to police by a 2nd-grader, police said Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Teacher and a teenage student killed in a shooting at a Christian school in Wisconsin
item.description: A shooting at a Wisconsin Christian school occurred in a study hall and was reported to police by a 2nd-grader, police said Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Jill Jacobson, actress who appeared on 'Star Trek' and 'Falcon Crest,' dies at 70
item.description: "We are incredibly sad to say goodbye to our beautiful, soulful, hysterically funny, elegantly raunchy client, Jill Jacobson," her manager Ben Padua told Nexstar.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, ABC 4

item.title: Beauty store owner killed while trying to defend business from shoplifters
item.description: A Florida mom and business owner was reportedly killed while trying to stop shoplifters at her beauty supply store, leaving behind two daughters just weeks before the holidays.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mysterious drones leave officials perplexed
item.description: With drone sightings spreading through the East Coast, federal officials are helping local and state authorities.
item.dc:creator: Basil John, WGN 9

item.title: Romney: ‘You can’t complain’ if Trump does what he says he would do
item.description: “Donald Trump won. He won overwhelmingly. He said what he was going to do, and that’s what he’s doing,” Romney said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4

item.title: 5 Senate Republicans who could face primaries in 2026
item.description: Republican senators are gearing up for the possibility of primary challenges ahead of the 2026 midterms as they seek to navigate the new political environment of a second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, WGN 9

item.title: California man dies after being mauled by his own dogs: police
item.description: A man was mauled by his three dogs at a neighborhood park Thursday afternoon, according to the San Diego Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Tony Shin, KTLA 5

item.title: What we know about drones over New Jersey, other parts of the US
item.description: The drones have prompted concern from local residents, anger from lawmakers and endless speculation on social media.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, Fox 5

item.title: Texas company says baby poop and fungi could be the solution to plastic waste
item.description: A dirty diaper may be gross to you, but one Texas company believes it could be the secret ingredient to helping answer an environmental question: How do we clean up all of our plastic waste?
item.dc:creator: Eric Henrikson, Fox 5

item.title: ABC agrees to give $15 million to Donald Trump's presidential library to settle defamation lawsuit
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — ABC News has agreed to pay $15 million toward Donald Trump’s presidential library to settle a defamation lawsuit over anchor George Stephanopoulos' inaccurate on-air assertion that the president-elect had been found civilly liable for raping writer E. Jean Carroll.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Millionaire boomers less likely to share their wealth: Charles Schwab
item.description: Data revealed that baby boomers, born from 1946 to 1964, plan to spend most of their wealth rather than reserve it for future generations.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Blake Horvath shines as Navy dominates in a 31-13 victory over Bryson Daily and No. 19 Army
item.description: LANDOVER, Md. (AP) — This was an Army-Navy game unlike any before it. The teams had combined for 19 victories this season before their annual clash, a record for the rivalry. Of course, only one of them would be responsible for No. 20 — the biggest win of them all. “I think they're like us.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Holiday shopping scams to avoid this winter
item.description: Waiting on a plethora of present-related notifications? Don't assume all emails and texts you get are legitimate.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KRON4

item.title: Trump leans on czars for key policy areas
item.description: President-elect Trump is signaling he plans to lean on appointed czars to carry out his plans in key policy areas, specifically when it comes to immigration and energy production, centralizing power within the White House.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: 6 ways to avoid a financial holiday hangover
item.description: The festivity of December is replaced all too quickly by the due dates of January, when the bills from holiday spending and travel arrive.
item.dc:creator: Kimberly Palmer, Fox 5

item.title: A look back at the most notable bankruptcies of 2024
item.description: This year saw its fair share of bankruptcies, with household names and century-old companies in the mix.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, ABC 4

item.title: Storms encase Iowa and eastern Nebraska in ice and generate rare tornado warning in San Francisco
item.description: A major ice storm created treacherous driving conditions across Iowa and eastern Nebraska this weekend and prompted temporary closures of Interstate 80 after numerous cars and trucks slid off the road.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, KRON4

item.title: Duke lacrosse players' rape accuser admits she lied
item.description: A woman who accused three former Duke University men’s lacrosse players of rape nearly two decades ago admitted she lied about the allegations and asked them for forgiveness.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Dick Vitale says he's cancer-free after 4 bouts with disease in just over 3 years
item.description: Vitale had surgery in the summer to remove cancerous lymph nodes from his neck. He was previously treated for melanoma and lymphoma, and had radiation treatments last year for vocal cord cancer.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: These are the Trump appointees who have faced legal troubles
item.description: President-elect Trump has named several people to his next administration who have faced legal troubles — including Trump himself, who was convicted in a New York hush money case during his 2024 campaign.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9
